description: Tolkien's legendarium adapted across seven decades of film, animation, games, and television — from the 1977 Rankin/Bass special through Peter Jackson's two trilogies, Amazon's Rings of Power, and an enormous catalog of licensed games, mobile titles, and fan projects stretching from 1982 to present.

media.title: The Hobbit (Rankin/Bass)
media.description: Animated TV film — Bilbo and Thorin's company journey to the Lonely Mountain. First screen adaptation of Tolkien, with songs by Maury Laws.
media.notes: Jellyfin. TV special but film-length. Pairs with the 1980 Rankin/Bass Return of the King.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings (Ralph Bakshi)
media.description: Rotoscoped animated film covering Fellowship through roughly the midpoint of The Two Towers. Ends abruptly — the planned sequel was never made.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Famously unfinished. The 1980 Rankin/Bass film serves as a rough conclusion.

media.title: The Return of the King (Rankin/Bass)
media.description: Animated TV film covering the Frodo/Sam arc and the fall of Sauron. Completes what Bakshi left unfinished. Tonally lighter with memorable songs.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Spiritual sequel to the 1977 Hobbit special.

media.title: The Hobbit (Melbourne House, text adventure)
media.description: Landmark text adventure with Inglish natural-language parsing. Thorin the AI companion will sit down and sing if left alone. One of the earliest licensed Tolkien games.
media.notes: DOSBox. Historically important for its NLP ambitions.

media.title: Lord of the Rings: Game One (Melbourne House)
media.description: Text adventure adapting Fellowship of the Ring. First part of a planned trilogy — Games Two and Three were never released after the company was sold.
media.notes: DOSBox. Defunct / incomplete. Curiosity only.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings, Vol. I (Interplay)
media.description: Party-based RPG adapting Fellowship. Real-time pausable combat. SNES and Genesis ports arrived in 1994. Ambitious for its era.
media.notes: Core: snes9x (SNES), genesis_plus_gx (GEN). DOSBox for PC original.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings, Vol. II: The Two Towers (Interplay)
media.description: Sequel covering The Two Towers. Vol. III was cancelled after Interplay lost the Tolkien license — the series ends mid-story.
media.notes: DOSBox. Vol. III never released. Series ends abruptly.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: Fellowship of the Ring (GBC)
media.description: GBC action-RPG released alongside the Peter Jackson film. Plays more as its own take on the book than a strict film tie-in.
media.notes: Core: gambatte.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: Fellowship of the Ring (Surreal Software)
media.description: Action-adventure using the book license — visually distinct, slower-paced, more atmospheric than the EA games. Underappreciated at launch.
media.notes: PCSX2 for PS2. Book-licensed, not Jackson film-licensed.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers (EA)
media.description: Hack-and-slash using Jackson film footage and assets. The first EA film tie-in; co-op on console. GBA port is a separate isometric game.
media.notes: Core: mgba (GBA). PCSX2 for PS2.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King (EA)
media.description: Sequel adding Gandalf, Faramir, Pippin, and Merry as playable. Three-player co-op. The best of the EA action games. GBA port is its own separate isometric game.
media.notes: Core: mgba (GBA). PCSX2 for PS2. Three-player co-op.

media.title: The Hobbit (Vivendi / Inevitable Entertainment)
media.description: Action-adventure based on the Tolkien book with a whimsical tone and platforming sections. Not related to the Jackson Hobbit films (which came a decade later).
media.notes: Core: mgba (GBA). PCSX2 for PS2. Book license.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: War of the Ring
media.description: RTS in the Warcraft III mold with Good and Evil campaigns. Book-licensed — visually distinct from the EA games. Underrated and largely forgotten.
media.notes: PC. Book license.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Battle for Middle-earth
media.description: RTS built on the Jackson film license. Good and Evil campaigns with base-building and hero units. Highly regarded in the RTS community. Officially delisted from all stores.
media.notes: PC only. Officially delisted. Community T3A patch exists. One of the best LotR games made.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Third Age (EA)
media.description: Turn-based JRPG following six original characters who shadow the Fellowship through all three films. EA's most unusual LotR game.
media.notes: Core: mgba (GBA). PCSX2 for PS2.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Battle for Middle-earth II
media.description: Sequel adding Elves and Dwarves as full factions, a War of the Ring conquest mode, and the Rise of the Witch-king expansion covering Angmar.
media.notes: PC only. Officially delisted. T3A:Online community patch restores multiplayer. Rise of the Witch-king included.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings Online
media.description: MMORPG set in Middle-earth during the events of the books. Still live as of 2026. The longest-running active Tolkien game. Free-to-play base with paid expansions.
media.notes: PC. Still active. F2P base game. Mines of Moria and Riders of Rohan expansions recommended.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings Online: Mines of Moria
media.description: First major LotRO expansion. Delves into Khazad-dûm with legendary item system and two new classes. Often cited as the game's creative peak.
media.notes: PC. Paid expansion for LotRO base game.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: Conquest
media.description: Battlefield-style class-based action game recreating key LotR battles. Evil campaign lets you rewrite history — Sauron wins. Online servers permanently shut down.
media.notes: Online servers defunct. Single-player still playable. Pandemic's final game before closure.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: Aragorn's Quest
media.description: Child-friendly action game retelling the trilogy through Sam telling his children the story. E-rated. Best on Wii for motion controls.
media.notes: Budget/family tier.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: War in the North
media.description: Three-player co-op action RPG following Eradan, Andriel, and Farin in the northern campaign against Sauron's forces. M-rated, original characters.
media.notes: PC/Steam. Three-player co-op. Underrated.

media.title: Lego Lord of the Rings
media.description: Full Lego treatment of the Jackson trilogy with film audio clips. Open Middle-earth hub world. One of the stronger Lego games.
media.notes: Officially delisted from all digital storefronts 2023. Physical copies still work.

media.title: Guardians of Middle-earth
media.description: Console MOBA featuring characters from across Tolkien's legendarium. Servers permanently shut down 2016.
media.notes: Defunct. Servers dead. No meaningful offline mode.

media.title: The Hobbit: Kingdoms of Middle-earth
media.description: Mobile city-builder strategy tie-in to the Jackson Hobbit films. F2P with heavy pay-to-win. Servers shut down 2018.
media.notes: Mobile. Defunct. Servers dead as of 2018.

media.title: Lego The Hobbit
media.description: Lego game covering the first two Hobbit films — the third was never integrated despite DLC. Oddly incomplete due to licensing timing.
media.notes: Officially delisted from all digital storefronts 2023. Physical copies still work.

media.title: Middle-earth: Shadow of Mordor
media.description: Open-world action RPG introducing the Nemesis System. Talion and the wraith Celebrimbor hunt the Black Captains. Set in the gap between The Hobbit and LotR.
media.notes: PC/Steam. Partially delisted on console storefronts. Physical still works. Sequel required to complete Talion's arc.

media.title: Middle-earth: Shadow of War
media.description: Sequel expanding the Nemesis System. Talion forges a new Ring of Power to assault Mordor. Larger world, Nazgûl origin stories, controversial loot-box ending later patched out.
media.notes: PC/Steam. Partially delisted digitally. Play Shadow of Mordor first.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: Adventure Card Game
media.description: Digital co-op card game in Early Access from 2018. Studio shut down 2020; game abandoned entirely. The physical LCG it's based on remains excellent.
media.notes: Defunct. Officially abandoned 2020.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: Rise to War
media.description: Mobile strategy with seasonal faction-vs-faction wars across a full map of Middle-earth. F2P with heavy gacha. Still live as of 2026.
media.notes: Mobile. iOS + Android. Still active.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: Gollum
media.description: Stealth platformer following Gollum between The Hobbit and LotR. Critically panned on release; Daedalic cancelled all future game development as a direct consequence.
media.notes: Commercially and critically disastrous. Curiosity play only.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: Heroes of Middle-earth
media.description: Mobile RPG in the Galaxy of Heroes mold. Collect characters from the films, books, and Rings of Power. F2P gacha. Still live as of 2026.
media.notes: Mobile. iOS + Android. Still active.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: Return to Moria
media.developer: Free Range Games / North Beach Games
media.description: Survival crafting in Khazad-dûm set in the Fourth Age. Dwarves reclaim Moria from darkness. Up to 8-player co-op.
media.notes: PC/Epic. Set in the Rings of Power Fourth Age continuity.

media.title: Tales of the Shire
media.description: Cozy life-sim in the Shire. Cook, garden, befriend hobbits. No combat whatsoever. Uses Weta Workshop's original bespoke Middle-earth designs.
media.notes: Released early 2025.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
media.description: Peter Jackson's adaptation of the first book. Frodo inherits the One Ring and flees the Shire with the Fellowship. Extended Edition adds ~30 minutes.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Extended Edition strongly recommended.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers
media.description: The Fellowship breaks. Aragorn's path to Helm's Deep, Frodo and Sam's journey with Gollum. Extended Edition adds vital Faramir and Ents scenes.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Extended Edition essential.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
media.description: The war for Middle-earth reaches its climax. Won Best Picture and 10 other Oscars. Extended Edition adds the Mouth of Sauron and Saruman's end.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Extended Edition is the definitive cut. 11 Oscars total.

media.title: The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey
media.description: First of Jackson's three-film Hobbit adaptation. Bilbo joins Thorin's company. The Riddles in the Dark sequence is exceptional.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Consider the Tolkien Edit fan-cut for a tighter experience.

media.title: The Hobbit: The Desolation of Smaug
media.description: Mirkwood, Laketown, and the most cinematic dragon sequence in film history. Benedict Cumberbatch as Smaug is a highlight.
media.notes: Jellyfin.

media.title: The Hobbit: The Battle of the Five Armies
media.description: The battle for Erebor, Thorin's dragon-sickness arc, and the farewell that connects back to the LotR trilogy. Extended Edition has the full White Council battle.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Extended Edition recommended.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: The War of the Rohirrim
media.description: Anime-style animated film directed by Kenji Kamiyama (Ghost in the Shell: SAC). Tells the story of King Helm Hammerhand and the siege that gave Helm's Deep its name. Set 183 years before the Fellowship.
media.notes: Jellyfin. Theatrical December 2024.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Hunt for Gollum (2026 film)
media.description: New Peter Jackson-produced film with Andy Serkis returning to direct and reprise Gollum. Part of a planned new theatrical LotR trilogy.
media.notes: Theatrical 2026. In production.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Rings of Power (S1)
media.description: Amazon's Second Age adaptation. Galadriel hunts Sauron, the Rings of Power are forged, and Númenor rises. S1 ends with the creation of Mordor.
media.notes: Prime Video. 8 episodes.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Rings of Power (S2)
media.description: Sauron's true identity is revealed. The Rings for Men are forged. Darker tone and stronger characterisation than S1 — better received.
media.notes: Prime Video. 8 episodes.

media.title: The Hunt for Gollum (Fan Film)
media.description: Fan film by Chris Bouchard depicting Aragorn's hunt for Gollum between The Hobbit and Fellowship. Made for approximately £3000. Widely praised as the best LotR fan film ever made.
media.notes: Fan film. Free online. ~40 minutes. Ascended canon in cultural reputation.

media.title: Born of Hope (Fan Film)
media.description: Fan film depicting Arathorn and Gilraen — the parents of Aragorn — and Aragorn's early life as a Dúnedain heir. Companion piece to The Hunt for Gollum.
media.notes: Fan film. Free online. ~70 minutes. High production value.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: Middle-earth Defense
media.description: Tower defense game for iOS using Jackson film imagery. One of the first film-licensed iOS LotR games. No longer available on the App Store.
media.notes: iOS. Defunct. Removed from App Store.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: Legends of Middle-earth
media.description: Mobile RPG with turn-based combat. Collect heroes across the Tolkien legendarium. Launched 2022, servers shut down 2023.
media.notes: Mobile. Defunct. Servers shut down 2023.

media.title: The Tolkien Edit (Fan Cut — Hobbit Trilogy)
media.description: Fan edit by Maple Films compressing all three Hobbit films into a ~4-hour lean adaptation by removing non-Tolkien additions. Widely considered the definitive Hobbit viewing experience.
media.notes: Fan edit. Requires your own source files. Treat as ascended canon — the preferred way to watch the Hobbit trilogy.

media.title: Middle-earth: Shadow of Mordor (Mobile)
media.description: Mobile companion/spinoff tied to the Shadow of Mordor launch. Simplified combat with card-based mechanics. No longer available.
media.notes: Mobile. Defunct. Removed from storefronts.

media.title: The Lord of the Rings: War of the Rohirrim (Stage Musical Recording)
media.description: West End musical adaptation staged at Drury Lane 2007–2008. Originally Toronto 2006. Cast recording exists. Ambitious, technically complex, and commercially unsuccessful.
media.notes: Stage production. Cast recording available. Not a screen work.
